channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - wccftech.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for wccftech.com

item.title: Xbox Games Showcase 2026: Where To Watch, What to Expect To See On June 7
item.content:encoded:
The Xbox Games Showcase returns to the Summer Games Fest season this year, but while the format remains the same, everything at the company has changed. With a new leadership team, led by head Asha Sharma, and the goal of reclaiming fans' goodwill following the disastrous "Everything is an Xbox" campaign, this year's showcase could mark a new beginning for the entire brand.

Table of contents

  • Official Livestream
  • When and Where to Watch
  • Runtime
  • What to Expect

Official Livestream

You can watch the official Xbox Games Showcase 2026 livestream directly below when the broadcast goes live.

When and Where to Watch

The Xbox Games Showcase 2026 will broadcast live on Sunday, June 7, 2026 at 10am PT / 1pm ET / 6pm BST / 7pm CEST.

The event streams live across social and video platforms:

  • Official Xbox YouTube Channel
  • Official Xbox Twitch Channel
  • Official Xbox Facebook Profile

Runtime

While Microsoft has not explicitly locked in a final broadcast duration, based on previous years' events, the main Xbox Games Showcase is expected to run approximately 1 hour.

However, the showcase will be followed by a Gears of War: E-Day Direct, offering a dedicated deep dive into the next entry in the series launching later this year.

What to Expect

Although the only game originally confirmed for this year's Xbox Game Showcase is Gears of War: E-Day, on May 29, Xbox's Matt Booty confirmed that another look at Fable, which has been delayed to February 2027, will come during the show. Updates on Halo: Campaign Evolved and Call of Duty: Modern Warfare 4 are also expected.

Interestingly, a recent update to the Xbox Game Pass "Coming Soon" category may have revealed a few more games that will receive updates during the June 7 broadcast. These include:

  • Clockwork Revolution
  • Resonance: A Plague Tale Legacy
  • Witchbrook
  • Tropico 7
  • Armatus
  • There Are No Ghosts At The Grand

Beyond these, it is reasonable to expect plenty of third-party reveals. Following its reveal during last year's event, Persona 4 Revival is very likely to receive a new update during the broadcast, with a first proper look and possibly a release date, which may not be too far off. To give the spotlight entirely to the remake of the fourth entry in the series, it is unlikely Persona 6 will be announced during the event.

Unfortunately, those expecting more information on the Xbox Project Helix next-generation system will be disappointed. Xbox's Mat Booty set expectations on the showcase, saying that it will focus only on games.

What are your expectations for the Xbox Games Showcase 2026? Let us know in the comments below.

About the author: Francesco De Meo has been covering video games and technology since 2012, starting his career at small outlets like Gamersyndrome and GeekSnack. After joining Wccftech gaming section in 2015, he quickly expanded his video gaming coverage with in-depth reporting, interviews with iconic industry figures such as Grasshopper Manufacture founder and No More Heroes creator Goichi "Suda51" Suda, Resident Evil series creator Shinji Mikami, Team NINJA's president and Nioh series director Fumihiko Yasuda, and Silent Hill creator Keiichiro Toyama, reviews and on-the-ground coverage of major industry events such as Gamescom and E3. When he's not reporting or reviewing, Francesco can be found playing the genres he loves most, spending time with his six cats, reading, writing music, playing guitar and drumming for his progressive rock band.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/xbox-games-showcase-2026-where-to-watch-what-to-expect-to-see-on-june-7/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Subnautica 2 Early Access – How to Get Necrolei Cysts
item.content:encoded:
In Subnautica 2, you'll very quickly learn that resources like Copper, Titanium, and Quartz are the building blocks of what you'll need to do, well, anything, the more you progress in the game. You'll always need more of all three of those resources, and they're thankfully easy to find in every part of the map. What's not as easy to find, however, are Necrolei Cysts.

It's a resource that you'll need for a key crafted resource, Strong Acid, an ingredient you'll need to craft a Power Cell, which you'll need for when you want to craft your first Tadpole in the earlier stages of your playthrough. When you finally reach the point of upgrading your Tadpole, it'll be imperative again when you want to be able to power those upgrades with Entangled Power Cells.

You can craft Strong Acid with resources other than Necrolei Cysts, by putting Sulfur and Gold into a Processor, but those are resources more commonly found in areas you probably can't safely access yet by the time you're trying to craft a Power Cell for your first Tadpole.

That's why just using Ncrolei Cysts is the preferred option, both before and after you're capable of farming as much Sulfur and Gold as you want, frankly. This guide will show you where to find them, so you'll never be without.

For more on Subnautica 2, check out our tips and tricks guide, our guide on crafting your first Tadpole, and how to find Silver in the early game.

Subnautica 2 Early Access - How to Get Necrolei Cysts

The Old Habitat

The best place to find Necrolei Cysts is thankfully not too far from your Lifepod at the start of the game, as you essentially just have to head to the Old Habitat. Spotted much more easily at night, right next to the Old Habitat, is a bright purple forest where you'll find Necrolei Cysts growing.

You'll have found the Old Habitat by following the Blackboxes, but if not, you can find it by just heading north from the Lifepod, and it's about 375m away. Be sure to have your Survival Multi-tool with you, so you can cut them and farm them.

You just need two Necrolei Cysts to craft Strong Acid with a Processor, so the best thing to do is just grab as many as you can carry when you first go to farm them. You're not going to be using them often, especially in the beginning, so making one large trip should serve you for a while.

Right now, in the game's early access state, Necrolei Cysts are a very limited-use resource, though they could very well become more useful ahead of the game reaching 1.0. They're already pretty significant now, even if they're only good for making acid for Power Cells.

About the author: David has been writing about videogames, technology, and culture since 2020, with a focus on reporting daily news across multiple publications, including GameDaily.Biz, GameSkinny, and PlayStation Universe before joining Wccftech in 2025. David started contributing as Canada/US reporter for Wccftech's gaming section in 2025. Besides being up-to-date on the industry's movements, he loves interviewing developers, reviewing games, and writing intricate essays about the symbolism and layered meanings to be found in rich narratives as he's done for publications like GamesIndustry.Biz, LostInCult, and others. Outside of games he loves movies, music, theatre, his hometown, and his family, though not necessarily in that order.

Follow Wccftech on Google to get more of our news coverage in your feeds.



https://wccftech.com/how-to/subnautica-2-necrolei-cysts-how-to-find/
item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot
